description: A query with no supporting index scans the whole collection and degrades as data grows.
body: Failure mode: a find or aggregation $match on an unindexed field performs a COLLSCAN, reading every document, so latency grows linearly with collection size and the working set is evicted from cache. Detection signals: explain reports a COLLSCAN winning plan, totalDocsExamined hugely exceeds nReturned, and the slow-query log captures the operation. Do not ship a hot-path query without an index for its predicate. Safer fallback: add a compound index ordered by the ESR rule for the query, confirm explain flips to IXSCAN, and drop unused indexes flagged by $indexStats to keep write cost down.
